title: Week 3 Day 1 (1/2) — Hugging Face & Ollama
summary: Part 1 of 2 · What HF is (hub + libraries) vs Ollama · six libraries you'll use.

sections.heading: Week 3 goal
sections.remember: Move from **frontier APIs only** to **open-source models** you load and run yourself — mainly via **Hugging Face** and **Google Colab** GPUs.
points: You already: frontier models, assistants, tools (Weeks 1–2).

sections.heading: Hugging Face = two things
sections.remember: **HF Hub** (website) hosts **models**, **datasets**, **Spaces** — separate from **HF Python libraries** that run the code.
points: **Libraries:** open-source Python (PyTorch-first) to load weights, run, fine-tune **transformers**.

sections.heading: Ollama vs Hugging Face code
sections.remember: **Ollama** = packaged local app (GGUF, C++ server, OpenAI-compatible port). **HF** = real Python model code you can change and fine-tune.
points: Ollama (Week 1): fast pre-baked runner — you don't edit the neural net.
HF `transformers`: load weights in Jupyter/Colab — inspect layers, swap tokenizers, **fine-tune** later.
Use Ollama for quick local chat; use HF when you need control or training.

sections.heading: Six HF libraries (course map)
sections.remember: Package **`huggingface_hub`** downloads from the website Hub — confusing name, different from the site itself.

cheatSheet: HF Hub ≠ HF libraries (same brand, different layer)
Ollama = app runner · HF = code + weights
transformers is the main library this week
